items.title: Police open probe into delayed burial at Hulu Kelang Muslim cemetery
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have opened a criminal investigation into the alleged forgery of a signature linked to the delayed burial of a deceased person at the Ukay Perdana Muslim Cemetery in Hulu Kelang. Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) director Datuk Rusdi Mohd Isa said an investigation paper had been opened under Section 468 of the Penal Code for forgery for the purpose of cheating. The latest development comes after Ampang Jaya police confirmed they had received a report from the deceased's family over delays in the burial, which recently went viral on social media.

items.title: Johor polls: BN’s mix of experience, fresh faces key to governing state, says Khaled Nordin
items.summary: KLUANG, June 28 — Barisan Nasional’s (BN) blend of experienced leaders and fresh faces for the 16th Johor state election is the best formula for meeting the increasingly complex challenges of governing, Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said. He said criticism that BN was relying heavily on ‘recycled’ candidates should be viewed in the context of the need to ensure continuity in leadership and experience within the state administration. According to Mohamed Khaled, fielding an entirely new line-up would amount to an unnecessary gamble at a time when experienced leaders are needed to navigate increasingly complex challenges.

items.title: China mulls revisions to gold import, export regulations
items.summary: BEIJING: Chinese regulators have proposed revisions to current regulations on the import and export of gold and gold products, aiming to streamline administration, facilitate trade, and improve the management of gold carried across the border by individuals, reported Xinhua. Under the draft rules, the provision requiring the PBOC and customs authorities to jointly formulate rules for individuals carrying or mailing gold and gold products across the border would be removed. To further optimise management, the draft also states that such cross-border movements will remain subject to customs supervision.

items.title: 'Why did you leave so soon?' Widow mourns husband killed in expressway crash
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The widow of a motorcyclist who was killed after plunging about 30m from the Setiawangsa-Pantai Expressway (SPE) has shared an emotional tribute to her late husband, saying she is struggling to come to terms with raising their children alone. In a post on Threads, the woman, known only as Nurbaizura, shared what she described as the last photograph of their family together and expressed her grief four days after her husband's death. "Al-Fatihah to my husband, my soulmate, Muhammad Shafeeq Hashim," she wrote.

items.title: Myvi in 3-vehicle crash had broken down on emergency lane before hit by lorry
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: Police confirmed that a Perodua Myvi involved in an accident this morning had stopped on the emergency lane due to a breakdown, before it was rear-ended by a lorry. Kuala Muda district police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan said the 9am accident involved the Perodua Myvi, a Toyota Noah, and a lorry at Km94 of the North-South Expressway (southbound) near here. "Initial investigations revealed that the Toyota Noah had stopped in front of the stalled Perodua Myvi.

items.title: Migrants pay up to RM6,000 each to be smuggled into Malaysia by sea, says MMEA
items.summary: PASIR PUTEH: Migrants are believed to be paying agents up to RM6,000 each to be smuggled by sea into Malaysia for work, the Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) said. Kelantan MMEA director Kamal Mohamad said the migrants comprised five men and three women, aged between 18 and 51, who claimed to have paid RM6,000 each for a four-day sea journey to secure factory jobs. We then moved in and arrested eight undocumented migrants," he said at a press conference at the Kelantan MMEA headquarters in Tok Bali today.

items.title: Chery boosts presence in Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Chinese automaker Chery says it has expanded its presence in Malaysia to 60 dealerships nationwide and launched its 10th vehicle model, three years after re-entering the market. A key milestone in Chery's expansion is the development of the RM2.2 billion Chery Smart Auto Industrial Park in Lembah Beringin, Selangor. "The automotive industry thrives on competition, and we welcome fair competition," Chery said.

items.title: DPM Fadillah: Proposal for 17 new Sarawak seats unlikely this year as EC procedures ongoing
items.summary: KUCHING, June 28 — The proposal to add 17 new seats to the Sarawak Legislative Assembly is unlikely to be tabled in the Dewan Rakyat this year, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof. He said the matter must go through several processes and procedures under the Election Commission (EC), including public display exercises to allow members of the public to submit objections or feedback. “At the earliest, the EC can first display it to the public.

items.title: Johor polls: Pas says no cooperation with Bersatu despite PN ties
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Pas has denied claims that it is backing Bersatu in the Johor election, insisting the party is merely honouring seat allocations within Perikatan Nasional (PN) despite the absence of political cooperation on the ground. Pas vice-president Datuk Mohd Amar Nik Abdullah said there was no question of the party supporting Bersatu, as Bersatu remained a component party of PN. Bersatu is still a member of PN.

items.title: Onn Hafiz: Pas' call to back BN part of party's strategy
items.summary: SIMPANG RENGGAM: All political parties are free to determine their own election strategies, including Pas' call for its members to support BN candidates in constituencies not contested by Perikatan Nasional (PN). Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, who is also the caretaker menteri besar, said the matter was entirely up to the respective parties. Onn Hafiz was responding to reports that Pas had instructed its members and supporters to vote for Umno or BN candidates in state seats where PN is not contesting in the Johor election.

items.title: Budi Madani portal receives 13,000 new applications as of 11am: Finance Ministry
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The official Budi Madani portal has received over 13,000 new applications as of 11 am today for an additional 100 litres of subsidised diesel per month for eligible private pick-up truck and jeep owners. The Ministry of Finance (MOF), in a statement today, said this addition increases the total diesel subsidy quota to 300 litres per month for eligible vehicle owners. "The first day of the early access rollout for the Budi Madani Diesel (BUDI Diesel) initiative for eligible private diesel vehicle owners in Peninsular Malaysia on June 27 ran smoothly.

items.title: Onn Hafiz says open debates not Johor’s way after offer from Maszlee Malik
items.summary: SIMPANG RENGGAM, June 28 — Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has reportedly said open debates between candidates may not be suitable for the Johor state election, adding that they could disrupt the harmony of the campaign period. According to Buletin TV3 , he said Johor’s political culture has long placed importance on decorum, restraint and orderly debate, rather than open exchanges that could turn confrontational. This comes after Pakatan Harapan’s candidate for the Puteri Wangsa seat, Datuk Maszlee Malik, had expressed his willingness to debate any party, particularly Onn Hafiz, during the Johor campaign period.

items.title: Golok 'fun' trip ends in arrest for two men over illegal entry at Tumpat jetty
items.summary: TUMPAT: Two men were detained by the General Operations Force (GOF) after they were caught entering the country from Thailand via an illegal jetty in Kampung Mentua here yesterday evening. GOF Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said his team spotted the two men on a boat approaching the riverbank while conducting surveillance at the location. "Both men were detained.

items.title: Bank Muamalat unveils initiatives to boost women participation in Islamic finance
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd has launched two new initiatives aimed at strengthening Islamic financial inclusion, introducing a women-focused takaful solution and a digital inheritance planning tool in conjunction with the Karnival Celik Kewangan Terengganu 2026. The initiatives comprise M Zahra Takaful MyLadies, a dedicated protection plan for women, and a Digital Faraid Calculator developed in collaboration with As-Salihin Trustee Bhd. Bank Muamalat said the launches reflect its ongoing commitment to expanding access to Islamic financial solutions that are more inclusive, practical and aligned with current societal needs, particularly in areas such as financial protection, family wellbeing and legacy planning.

items.title: Johor polls: BN's mix of experience, fresh faces key to governing - Khaled
items.summary: KLUANG: Barisan Nasional's (BN) blend of experienced leaders and fresh faces for the 16th Johor state election is the best formula for meeting the increasingly complex challenges of governing, Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said. He said criticism that BN was relying heavily on 'recycled' candidates should be viewed in the context of the need to ensure continuity in leadership and experience within the state administration. He said fielding an entirely new line-up would amount to an unnecessary gamble at a time when experienced leaders are needed to navigate increasingly complex challenges.

items.title: Jakim seeks tighter vetting of Quranic, Hadith content in books
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Islamic Development Department (Jakim) has urged the National Book Council of Malaysia to remind publishers to exercise greater care when publishing religious books containing Quranic and Hadith texts. Director‑general Datuk Dr Sirajuddin Suhaimee said in a statement today that the department, together with state Islamic religious authorities, was ready to provide guidance to publishers of religious books to ensure publications comply with syariah requirements. He said the department supported the firm stance taken by Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan, who had urged publishers to preserve the sanctity of Islam and ensure religious books are reviewed by the relevant authorities.

items.title: Public hospitals can't bear healthcare burden alone, says deputy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Public hospitals cannot shoulder the country's healthcare needs alone, says Deputy Health Minister Datuk Hanifah Hajar Taib. She said while hospitals remain the backbone of the healthcare system, a strong primary care network, including private clinics, is crucial to achieving better health outcomes and long-term sustainability. "Malaysia's healthcare system must become more sustainable and responsive to the changing needs of the population," she said at a memorandum of understanding (MoU) signing between the Malaysian Medical Association, IHH Healthcare Malaysia and Monash University Malaysia.

items.title: Johor polls: PH flag burned in Tanjung Surat, report lodged
items.summary: He said sabotage should not have a place in a mature democracy. A report was lodged with the Election Commission (EC) and the police, he said in a statement. He said political differences must never be translated into acts of provocation or vandalism, stressing that such behaviour erodes democratic values.

items.title: Malaysia shortlists four countries for new LCS anti-ship missile system, says defence minister
items.summary: KLUANG, June 28 — The Ministry of Defence (Mindef) has shortlisted four countries as possible suppliers of an anti-ship missile system for the Littoral Combat Ship (LCS) project after Norway cancelled the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) procurement. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the selection process will focus on key criteria to ensure the chosen system meets the operational needs of the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) beyond basic specifications. Mohamed Khaled said the government is reviewing the delivery timeline to avoid delays that could affect the country’s maritime defence capabilities.

items.title: DNA results of DUKE crash victims expected tomorrow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: DNA test results to confirm the identities of the two university students killed in a fiery crash on the Duta-Ulu Kelang Expressway (DUKE) are expected to be ready tomorrow. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said investigators were awaiting the outcome of the deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) analysis. "The DNA results are expected tomorrow," he said when contacted today.

items.title: 6 students held for bullying after 14-year-old quits school in Muar
items.summary: MUAR: Police have arrested six Form Five students over the bullying and extortion of a 14-year-old hostel student who was left traumatised and quit school. The Form Two student lodged a police report at 5pm yesterday, alleging he was assaulted by senior students at a school here in May. Police arrested the six 17-year-old suspects in a series of raids in Muar and Alor Gajah, Melaka, between 1.30am and 2.12am today.

items.title: Govt mulls affordable housing on LRT3 park-and-ride sites to boost public transport use
items.summary: KLANG, June 28 — The government is planning to redevelop large park-and-ride sites along the newly launched LRT3 Shah Alam Line into affordable housing and commercial projects, in a move aimed at making better use of public land while encouraging greater use of public transport. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the proposal would focus on selected stations with sizable parking facilities, rather than every station along the line, with Sri Andalas station serving as the initial example. He said Prasarana had identified large parcels of land around some stations that are currently used solely as surface car parks, despite their development potential.

items.title: Affordable housing, retail spaces planned along Shah Alam LRT3 line
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The government is looking into developing land owned by Prasarana around the Shah Alam LRT3 line stations under a Transit-Oriented Development (TOD) plan, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said that he wanted proposals for housing development on Prasarana land in Shah Alam to be processed efficiently so residents could benefit soon. "For this Prasarana land project, I want to see a proposal processed efficiently so that people in Shah Alam can get housing soon," he said.

items.title: Where are they? Dogs disappear before South Korea meat ban
items.summary: The deserted site in Pyeongtaek, south of Seoul, offers a glimpse into an industry rapidly disappearing as South Korea's landmark dog meat ban enters into force next year. Historically regarded as a summer stamina booster – particularly among older and rural people – dog meat has steadily fallen out of favour as younger Koreans embrace dogs as pets and public attitudes shift. Government figures show between 400,000 and 450,000 dogs were being raised for meat in 2024.

items.title: Penang, Johor lead export surge as Malaysia's trade hits RM327.6b in May
items.summary: Penang was the standout performer, recording a RM50.0 billion increase in exports, followed by Johor (RM6.6 billion), Kuala Lumpur (RM2.1 billion), Sarawak (RM1.7 billion), Perak (RM504.4 million), Terengganu (RM346.0 million) and Kelantan (RM35.7 million). However, exports declined in several states including Sabah (RM1.1 billion), Pahang (RM905.8 million), Melaka (RM569.7 million), Selangor (RM509.1 million), Labuan (RM282.9 million), Kedah (RM183.4 million), Negri Sembilan (RM154.1 million) and Perlis (RM10.3 million). Other states registering import growth were Kedah (RM1.3 billion), Negri Sembilan (RM1.0 billion), Kuala Lumpur (RM496.4 million), Sabah (RM245.0 million), Pahang (RM68.1 million), Labuan (RM31.5 million) and Terengganu (RM2.2 million).

items.title: Fadillah: 17 additional Sarawak state seats unlikely before next election
items.summary: KUCHING: The proposed addition of 17 seats to the Sarawak state assembly is unlikely to be tabled in Parliament before the next state election, says Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof. Fadillah said the Election Commission (EC) informed him that several processes related to the redelineation exercise must be completed before the matter could be brought to the Dewan Rakyat. Fadillah said the proposal is expected to be tabled in Parliament next year.

items.title: Police arrest four men, seize roosters and gambling items in Mukah cockfighting raid
items.summary: MUKAH, June 28 — Four local men were arrested for allegedly involved in cockfighting activities during a police raid at an open area within an oil palm plantation along Jalan Bayan Ulu here yesterday. Mukah district police chief DSP Muhamad Rizal Alias said the raid was carried out at about 2pm by officers from the Criminal Investigation Division of the Mukah District Police Headquarters (IPD Mukah), following intelligence and public information. “Police also seized three live roosters, one dead chicken, two cockfighting spurs, a weighing scale, various gambling paraphernalia, and RM55 in cash,” he said.

items.title: Serbian President Vucic says he will resign within weeks amid student protests
items.summary: BELGRADE: Serbian President Aleksandar Vucic said on Saturday he would resign within weeks and the country will hold early presidential and parliamentary elections, following 18 months of anti-government protests. Vucic's second and final mandate was due to expire in mid-2027. Vucic said he would help his Serbian Progressive Party (SNS) win the presidential election and the early parliamentary vote, also originally set for 2027.

items.title: Father’s role important in shielding children from social ills as Madani govt bolsters family‑focused policies, says DPM Fadillah
items.summary: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said efforts to empower parenting, particularly fathers’ involvement in children’s upbringing and education, must continue to be prioritised as part of national efforts to build stable families, a harmonious society and a safer future for the next generation. The text of his speech was read out by Deputy Women, Family and Community Development Minister Lim Hui Ying at the 2026 National Father’s Day celebration organised by the National Population and Family Development Board (LPPKN) here today. Fadillah said the Madani Government has always placed the family institution as the foundation of national strength, and in line with this aspiration, the National Family Compassion Policy (Dasar KASIH Keluarga Negara), launched earlier, serves as an important guide in strengthening family institutions in the country.

items.title: Khaled: BN's mix of new and old candidates the best formula
items.summary: KLUANG: Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin has dismissed claims that Barisan Nasional (BN) is fielding "recycled" candidates for the Johor election, saying the line-up reflects a balanced mix of experienced and new faces. He said the BN candidate list combines seasoned leaders and fresh candidates, describing it as the best formula to govern the state effectively. "If we field only new candidates, it would be like conducting an experiment.

items.title: Mindef looks to four countries for new anti-ship missiles after Norway export ban
items.summary: KLUANG: Malaysia has identified four potential countries as suppliers of anti-ship missile systems to replace the Norwegian naval strike missiles (NSM) after Norway cancelled its export licence to the country. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the ministry is evaluating offers from the four countries based on key criteria to ensure the chosen system meets the operational requirements of the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN), particularly for the Littoral Combat Ship (LCS). "We have four countries we are considering.

items.title: Google limits Meta's use of its Gemini AI models
items.summary: LONDON: Google has put limits on Meta's use of its Gemini AI models after the social media company sought more computing capacity than the rival tech group could provide, the Financial Times reported on Sunday. Google, owned by Alphabet, told Meta around March it could not meet the full Gemini capacity the company had sought to purchase, the newspaper said, adding that the shortfall disrupted and delayed some of Meta's internal AI projects. Meta has been particularly impacted due to its exceptionally high demand for Google's models, the FT said.

items.title: Kota Samarahan to serve as alternative venue for major state events as Kuching nears saturation, says state minister
items.summary: KOTA SAMARAHAN, June 28 — Kota Samarahan is being positioned as an alternative hub for major state events through the upcoming Samarahan Boulevard development, as Kuching approaches saturation, said Tourism, Creative Industry and Performing Arts Minister Datuk Seri Abdul Karim Rahman Hamzah. Abdul Karim, who is also Asajaya assemblyman said Kuching is increasingly congested when hosting large-scale events, particularly at Padang Merdeka. “Looking at Samarahan, which still has ample available land, I have proposed that the area be planned as a long-term venue for major state-level events,” he said when officiating the Samarahan Food and Cultural Festival here last night.

items.title: Three Pakistani troops killed in terror attack in Karachi
items.summary: KARACHI: Three Pakistani Rangers were killed in a terror attack in the southern megacity Karachi on Saturday evening, the country's military said, following an explosion and heavy gunfire. A statement released Sunday by the Pakistan Armed Forces said terror group Jamaat-ul-Ahrar was responsible for the "cowardly" raid on a Rangers camp in the port city. The statement added that three Rangers died and four more were wounded.

items.title: Onn Hafiz: Debates may fuel emotions during election campaign
items.summary: SIMPANG RENGGAM: Public debates are not the most suitable approach during an election campaign, as Johor's political culture has long been centred on harmony, decorum and mutual respect. Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman and caretaker menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said debates could sometimes trigger emotional reactions among supporters, which may not be conducive to a healthy election environment. "In Johor, we do not practise politics by engaging in verbal spats or public debates.

items.title: South Korea, Japan reaffirm denuclearisation goal, closer defence ties
items.summary: SEOUL: South Korea and Japan on Sunday reaffirmed their commitment to the denuclearisation of the Korean peninsula and agreed to revive joint search-and-rescue drills in a step forward for security ties between the neighbouring countries. Meeting in Seoul, South Korean Defence Minister Ahn Gyu-back and his Japanese counterpart Shinjiro Koizumi agreed to work on regional stability bilaterally, as well as through their partnerships with Washington, in the sixth round of talks between the two countries. South Korea and Japan, with U.S. encouragement, have been working to develop closer ties since 2022 and overcome sometimes bitter historical differences, a policy continued by President Lee Jae Myung and Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi.

items.title: Customs Dept seizes over RM800,000 in untaxed cigarettes in four raids in Terengganu and Kelantan
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, June 28 — The Royal Malaysian Customs Department (JKDM) seized white cigarettes and keretek (clove cigarettes) worth RM807,718.12, including taxes, in four separate raids conducted between April 16 and May 3. He said inspection of the premises uncovered 10,000 cigarettes worth an estimated RM9,020, including taxes. Inspection of the vehicle found 200,000 white cigarettes,” he said.

items.title: All aboard: Shah Alam LRT Line opens to public tomorrow
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The Shah Alam LRT Line will open to the public tomorrow, supported by a network of feeder buses and Rapid KL On-Demand van services, says Transport Minister Anthony Loke. Loke said the opening of the line marked a significant step in expanding a modern, integrated and world-class public transport network in the Klang Valley. "The opening of the Shah Alam Line completes another modern rail transport network connecting residential areas, commercial centres, educational institutions, hospitals and industrial areas in the western Klang Valley.

items.title: MMC rolls out Semak Berita to tackle AI-driven misinformation in Johor polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Media Council (MMC) has launched Semak Berita, a public initiative to help verify dubious, fabricated or misused election-related information during the Johor election period.S Chairperson of the council, Tan Sri Nallini Pathmanathan, said the initiative addresses growing concerns over the misuse of media identities during elections. MMC said when the names and logos of legitimate media organisations are used to spread falsehoods, the damage extends beyond a single newsroom. Public trust in the entire media ecosystem is undermined, MMC said.

items.title: Strong primary care key to sustainable healthcare system as hospitals face rising demand, says deputy health minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 28 — Strong primary care is essential to achieving better health outcomes and building a more sustainable healthcare system in Malaysia. Deputy Health Minister Datuk Hanifah Hajar Taib said hospitals alone could no longer shoulder the country’s growing healthcare demands, making collaboration among healthcare providers, academia and professional bodies increasingly important. “Malaysia’s healthcare system must become more sustainable and more responsive to changing population needs.

items.title: BN considers itself underdog in Johor polls, intensifies outreach ahead of July 11, says Onn Hafiz
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 28 — Barisan Nasional (BN) is approaching the 16th Johor state election as the underdog and will not take the contest for granted despite a smooth nomination process across all 56 seats and a strong show of support from party members. Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, who is also BN’s Machap candidate, said the coalition would continue to work hard and intensify its outreach efforts to engage as many voters as possible ahead of polling day on July 11. “That is the Johor way.

items.title: PM Anwar: Free LRT3 Shah Alam Line rides for one month starting tomorrow
items.summary: KLANG, June 28 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today announced that commuters can ride the newly launched LRT3 Shah Alam Line for free for a month beginning tomorrow. Anwar said the decision followed discussions between the Transport Ministry and the Finance Ministry, acknowledging that while the free rides would incur additional costs, the long-term benefits outweighed the expense. The Finance Ministry naturally looks at the additional costs involved,” he said.

items.title: ‘Thank you PAS’: Dr Akmal welcomes directive to back Umno-BN in Johor seats where Perikatan absent
items.summary: SHAH ALAM — Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Muhamad Akmal Saleh has welcomed PAS’ call for its members and supporters to vote for Umno-BN candidates in state seats not contested by PN in the 16th Johor state election. “Thank you PAS. PAS had reportedly issued the directive, with its deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man quoted as saying that he was confident party members understood the need for Muslim unity.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues thunderstorm warning for six states across Peninsular, Sabah and Sarawak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 28 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning for six states, with heavy rain and strong winds expected until 3pm today. In a statement, MetMalaysia said the affected areas in Peninsular Malaysia are Kuala Muda in Kedah; the Southwest, Northeast and Seberang Perai Utara districts in Penang; Seremban, Port Dickson and Rembau in Negeri Sembilan; and the whole of Melaka. In Sabah, the affected areas are Telupid, Beluran and Sandakan in the Sandakan division, as well as Pitas and Kudat in the Kudat division.

items.title: STAR Sabah Wiranita Bingkor leaders quit, citing party direction and women’s role
items.summary: KENINGAU, June 28 — Parti Solidariti Tanah Airku (STAR Sabah) Bingkor Division’s Wiranita chief Jennifer Epil, who also serves as the party’s central Wiranita vice executive committee member, has announced her resignation and withdrawal from the party with immediate effect. In a statement today, Jennifer said her decision was supported by several members of the STAR Sabah Wiranita Bingkor Division committee, who unanimously agreed to leave the party after careful and mature consideration. At the same time, Jennifer expressed her appreciation and gratitude to the STAR Sabah leadership for the trust, opportunities, and experience extended to her throughout her involvement in the party.

items.title: DOSM: 2026 Economic Census enumeration in Sabah passes 60pc mark amid strong stakeholder support
items.summary: To further improve the response rate, DOSM Sabah recently held an EC2026 engagement session with professional bodies and business associations at the Sabah International Convention Centre (SICC) here. In 2024, Sabah’s GDP stood at RM84.3 billion, with the services sector remaining the largest contributor at RM44.2 billion, followed by mining and quarrying (RM18.6 billion), agriculture (RM12.2 billion), manufacturing (RM6.1 billion) and construction (RM3 billion). “Based on the findings of the 2023 Economic Census, Sabah recorded 68,267 business establishments, generating a gross output of RM182.5 billion and value added of RM96.9 billion.

items.title: Flood evacuees fall to 16 in Selangor; Perak centre sees small rise to 52
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 28 — The situation continues to improve in the flood-hit area of Selangor, while Perak recorded a slight increase in the number of evacuees this morning. In SELANGOR,16 people from 10 families are being accommodated at a relief centre at Sekolah Kebangsaan (SK) RTB Bukit Changgang as at 8 am, compared to 309 people from 150 families last night. In PERAK, the number of evacuees still taking shelter at the Dewan Bandaran Gopeng in the Kampar district since June 20 rose slightly to 52 people from 16 families, compared with 51 people from 16 families last night.

items.title: Batik Air to launch twice‑daily Bintulu–KL flights from July 20, says tourism minister
items.summary: BINTULU, June 28 — The flight frequency between Bintulu and Kuala Lumpur will increase to twice daily beginning July 20, said Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing. According to the Bintulu MP, with the new schedule, passengers will have access to two daily flights from Kuala Lumpur to Bintulu, departing at 10am and 2.30pm. Flights from Bintulu to Kuala Lumpur will depart at 1.10pm and 5.40pm.

items.title: Kuching’s Tabuan Hilir blaze razes six houses, leaving 44 homeless
items.summary: KUCHING, June 28 — A total of 44 individuals from six families were left homeless after a fire razed their houses at Tabuan Hilir here in the early hours today. “The fire involved seven houses, six of which were completely destroyed, while one sustained slight damage. The blaze also completely destroyed three motorcycles.

items.title: Limbang sees up to half a million visitors; RM50m Santaba Jetty plan to boost ecotourism, says Sarawak deputy minister
items.summary: MIRI, June 28 — Tourism in Limbang continues to gain momentum, driven by rising visitor arrivals and the growing appeal of its unique mangrove ecosystem, with the Santaba Jetty area set for further development as a key ecotourism destination. In stating this, State Deputy Minister of Food Industry, Commodity and Regional Development Datuk Abdul Rahman Ismail said Limbang has recorded about 500,000 visitor arrivals, mainly from Brunei, as of June this year. Dr Abdul Rahman said the Santaba Jetty area, one of northern Sarawak’s emerging tourism attractions, will undergo further development to unlock its full potential.

items.title: PAS to decide support for Bersatu in Johor polls at special meeting tomorrow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 28 — PAS will decide tomorrow on its position regarding support for Bersatu a in the Johor state election, party secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan reportedly said. According to Sinar Harian , Takiyuddin said the decision would be made at a special PAS central committee meeting scheduled for 5pm. Takiyuddin said the meeting would also cover PAS’ political cooperation with parties in Perikatan Nasional.

items.title: National defence is everyone’s responsibility under Hanruh, says Sarawak minister
items.summary: MIRI, June 28 — The Total Defence (Hanruh) concept must be understood and embraced by every segment of Malaysian society, as safeguarding national security and sovereignty is not the sole responsibility of the Malaysian Armed Forces (ATM), the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) or enforcement agencies alone. According to Sarawak Transport Minister Datuk Seri Lee Kim Shin, the concept requires the involvement of the entire population through a ‘Whole of Government, Whole of Society’ (WoGoS) approach. He said the approach must continue to be strengthened in view of Malaysia facing increasingly complex security challenges including cyber threats, the spread of false information, cross-border crime, smuggling, human trafficking, maritime threats and natural disasters.

items.title: Once a sick project, SK Kampung Baru Kapit rebuild to open July 8 under Sarawak’s dilapidated schools programme
items.summary: KAPIT, June 28 — Once classified as a “dilapidated school”, SK Kampung Baru at Jalan Suhaili here is now completed under Sarawak’s RM1 billion Phase 1 Rebuild of Dilapidated Schools programme and set to officially open this July 8. During a visit by Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Openg, he was briefed by JKR Sarawak director Datuk Cassidy Morris that the project, which began in 2020 at a cost of RM12.84 million, was completed with the intervention of a rescue contractor appointed by the Public Works Department (JKR) Sarawak. Accompanying him were State Education, Innovation and Talent Development Minister Datuk Seri Roland Sagah Wee Inn, Deputy Minister Datuk Dr Annuar Rapaee, Sarawak Education Department director Omar Mahli, Kapit Education Officer Jarit Jarmit, SK Kampung Baru headmistress Seniah Othman and school staff.

items.title: Volkswagen Beetle believed destroyed in fire under Satok flyover
items.summary: KUCHING, June 28 — A classic car, believed to be a Volkswagen Beetle, parked beneath the Satok flyover here was destroyed in a fire early yesterday. “The fire was raging upon the firefighters’ arrival. “However, the car was completely destroyed,” said the Fire and Rescue Department (Bomba) in a statement.

items.title: Sarawak Immigration detains 18 Indonesians in Kota Padawan joint operation
items.summary: KUCHING, June 28 — The Sarawak Immigration Department detained 18 foreign nationals during a joint operation with the Padawan Municipal Council (MPP) around Mile 10, Kota Padawan, on Friday. “A total of 26 foreign nationals were checked to verify their travel documents and immigration status. “The operation resulted in the detention of 18 Indonesian nationals, comprising eight men and 10 women,” the statement said here yesterday.

items.title: Tourism Ministry sharpens strategy, targets China, Indonesia, Japan, India and key long‑haul markets
items.summary: IPOH, June 28 — The Ministry of Tourism, Arts and Culture (MOTAC) through Tourism Malaysia, is strengthening its strategy by focusing on high-performing and stable markets including China, Indonesia, Japan and India. Deputy Minister Chiew Choon Man said this included focusing on long-haul markets such as Russia, Germany and Australia to ensure market diversification. He said the ministry would also continue to work closely with industry stakeholders to address operational challenges and cost pressures.

items.title: Buy Malaysian Goods campaign rebranded as Jom Malaysia, says Armizan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 28 — Four decades after its introduction, the Buy Malaysian Goods campaign has been rebranded as Jom Beli Produk Malaysia (JOM MALAYSIA) to strengthen the branding of local products and services while fostering a spirit of economic patriotism among Malaysians. ““We want Jom Beli Produk Malaysia to become a national movement rooted in the values and spirit of patriotism, particularly economic patriotism,” he told reporters after launching the Jom Malaysia x Pesta Kita Festival at Central Market here yesterday. He said the initiative would adopt a whole-of-nation approach, involving collaboration among ministries, government agencies, industry players, traders and the public to comprehensively strengthen the use of locally made products.

items.title: Johor election: Zahid pledges full support for federal unity government if BN wins
items.summary: PONTIAN, June 27 — BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has assured that he will ensure that the Johor State Government moves in line and provides full support to the federal government if it is given a mandate in the Johor state election. “I assure you that if the mandate is given again, the government in Johor state led by our friend will continue to work together with the central government for the benefit of the entire Johor people, God willing,” he said at the Johor State Kemas Staff Meeting at Pontian MARA Junior Science College here, today. Ahmad Zahid, who is also deputy prime minister, also expressed his commitment to ensure full cooperation between the state administration and Putrajaya in order to strengthen the existing Unity Government.

items.title: Selangor religious dept denies mosque caused Ukay Perdana burial delay — blames family confusion and NGO
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 27 — The Selangor Islamic Religious Department (Jais) has denied allegations that a delay in the burial process at the Islamic Cemetery in Ukay Perdana, Hulu Klang, which went viral last Thursday, was caused by the Nurul Hidayah Mosque in Kampung Pandan Dalam. Jais director Datuk Mohd Shahzihan Ahmad said the delay stemmed from confusion among the family of the deceased over burial arrangements, which also involved a non-governmental organisation (NGO), the Salatulrahim Welfare Organisation (BKS). Mohd Shahzihan explained that the deceased’s father later met the Assistant Manager of Nurul Hidayah Mosque at about 10.30 am to obtain the Islamic Burial Application Form required for burial at the cemetery and agreed for the mosque to take over funeral arrangements from BKS.

items.title: Jais raids Petaling Jaya premise over Shiite activities — 226 foreign nationals questioned
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 27 — The Selangor Islamic Religious Department (Jais) raided a premise at Jalan Pusat Perdagangan Lagoon Perdana in Petaling Jaya here, which was believed to be conducting activities contrary to fatwa and linked to Shiite teachings, two days ago. Jais director Datuk Mohd Shahzihan Ahmad said the raid was conducted at 9.34 pm following public tip-offs, during which 226 foreign nationals comprising 147 men, 51 women and 28 children were inspected. Jais also advises Muslims not to be involved in any activities related to Shiite teachings,” he said.
